things.name: Schlossberg
things.description: Forested hilltop fortress rising 123m above the old town with the iconic 16th-century Clock Tower, Graz's most beloved symbol

things.name: Historic Centre of Graz (UNESCO)
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage old town with an extraordinary concentration of Renaissance and Baroque architecture in Central Europe

things.name: Kunsthaus Graz
things.description: Extraordinary biomorphic contemporary art museum nicknamed the Friendly Alien, a bold landmark on the Mur riverbank

things.name: Murinsel
things.description: Futuristic floating island café and event space in the middle of the Mur River, designed by Vito Acconci for Graz 2003 Cultural Capital

things.name: Eggenberg Palace
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage baroque palace of 1635 with 24 state apartments, hunting museum and a deer park on the city's western edge

things.name: Hauptplatz
things.description: Graz's grand central square with the Erzherzog-Johann-Brunnen fountain, baroque town hall and pavement cafes at the city's heart

things.name: Landesmuseum Joanneum
things.description: Austria's oldest public museum with encyclopedic collections spanning natural history, art and cultural history of Styria

things.name: Kaiser-Josef-Markt
things.description: Colourful daily farmers market where Graz residents buy fresh Styrian produce including world-famous pumpkin seed oil

things.name: Merkur Arena
things.description: Home stadium of Sturm Graz, the city's leading club, with a 16,000 capacity and a passionate atmosphere for Austrian Bundesliga

things.name: Schlossberg Lift and Clock Tower
things.description: Glass panoramic lift cut through the Schlossberg rock face offers the quickest route to the hilltop and its celebrated Clock Tower
